question: Q3: AI Beyond Coding

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used AI for architecture or project planning.
sections.hint: Examples: choosing state management, API design, splitting modules, drafting diagrams.
no_hands: What planning tasks do you feel AI cannot do well today?
If you tested AI here, what would you want it to deliver—outline, diagram, or options to compare?
Do you think architecture is too context-heavy for AI right now?
hands_up: What kinds of architecture help has AI given you—designing flows, state management, backend choices?
Did it save time in early planning?
How much did you trust AI’s suggestions before checking them?
Has AI ever surprised you with a useful alternative design?
follow_ups: Does AI help more with big-picture work or small refactors?
Who has combined AI planning with peer reviews to validate ideas?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used AI for UI or design work.
sections.hint: Examples: Figma AI, design-to-code tools, UI mockups, theme generators.
no_hands: What stops you from trying AI for design—lack of trust, weak results, or cost?
If you gave it one job, what would you want first—color palette, wireframes, or accessibility checks?
hands_up: Which AI design tools did you try—Figma AI, code-to-design, or something else?
How well did AI output translate into Flutter widgets?
Did AI help speed up iterations with designers or product managers?
Was there a moment where AI design suggestions felt more like noise than help?
follow_ups: Who has used AI mockups directly in Flutter code?
Anyone discover an AI trick that made handoff with designers smoother?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used AI to create or improve docs.
sections.hint: Examples: READMEs, inline comments, tutorials, auto-generated API docs.
no_hands: What holds you back—fear of mistakes, style issues, or lack of time?
Which doc tasks do you think AI might actually do well?
If you tried it, what would success look like—clearer READMEs, or faster onboarding?
hands_up: What kind of docs did AI help you with—tutorials, API guides, inline comments?
Did AI make your docs clearer for beginners?
Any moments where AI created misleading or bloated explanations?
Did AI save time on routine updates (e.g., parameter lists)?
follow_ups: Who double-checks AI-generated docs before publishing?
Has AI helped translate or localize docs for wider reach?

sections.prompt: Raise your hand if you’ve used AI for fast prototypes or hackathons.
sections.hint: Examples: throwaway code, proof-of-concept apps, early demos.
no_hands: What would keep you from using AI here—quality, speed, or setup time?
If you did use it, would you throw it away after demo, or refine it into real code?
hands_up: What’s the fastest prototype AI helped you create—minutes, hours?
Did AI make hackathons easier or just noisier?
Have you reused AI-generated code beyond the prototype stage?
What’s the biggest lesson learned about using AI for quick builds?
follow_ups: Did anyone build a prototype with AI that later grew into a real feature?
Has AI helped you test an idea faster than usual tools?

wrap_up: Which non-coding use case gave you the biggest benefit?
If you could improve one area—planning, design, docs, or prototyping—where would AI make the biggest difference?
